response:
To remove an interviewer (or any user) from an interview template:
1. Navigate to **Interviews > Templates**.
2. Click the **More options (⋮)** menu next to the template that the interviewer has access to and select **Share**.
3. In the **Share Interview Template** dialog, locate the interviewer’s name or team.
4. Choose **Remove User** from the drop‑down menu.
5. Confirm the removal in the confirmation dialog.
If you do not see the three‑dot menu for the user directly in the user list, ensure you are managing access via the template’s Share settings as described above.
justification: Section 4 provides step‑by‑step instructions for removing user access from an interview template, which applies to removing an interviewer.

response:
To pause your HackerRank subscription, follow these steps:
1. Click your profile icon (top‑right) and select **Settings**.
2. Open the **Billing** section under **Subscription**.
3. Click the **Cancel Plan** button. You will see a prompt about the new **Pause Subscription** feature.
4. Choose a pause duration (minimum 1 month, maximum 12 months) and click **Confirm Pause**.

Make sure you meet the prerequisites: the subscription must be active for at least 30 days and be a monthly Individual or Interview plan. (Source: Section 1 and Section 2 of the Pause Subscription documentation.)
justification: User request matches the documented 'How to Pause Your Subscription' procedure (Section 1) and prerequisites (Section 2). No escalation needed.

response:
Yes, you can update the name on your certificate. You may change it only once per account and the change will apply to all your certificates.

To update the name:
1. Open your certificate page.
2. In the **Full Name** field, enter the correct name.
3. Click **Regenerate Certificate**.
4. In the confirmation dialog, click **Update Name**.

After you complete these steps, the platform will update your certificate with the new name. Remember, you cannot change the name again after this update.
justification: The Certifications FAQs document states that the name can be updated once per account and provides the exact steps for updating the name on the certificate (Section 1, Certifications FAQs).

response:
To stop Claude’s crawler from accessing your site, add a robots.txt rule that blocks the ClaudeBot. Place the following lines in the robots.txt file at the top‑level of each domain or subdomain you want to opt out of:

```
User-agent: ClaudeBot
Disallow: /
```

(If you prefer to limit crawl rate rather than block entirely, you can use the Crawl‑delay directive instead, e.g., `Crawl-delay: 1`.)

After updating robots.txt, the Anthropic bots will honor the rule. If you suspect the bot is still crawling or have further questions, email claudebot@anthropic.com from an address that includes the affected domain.

You can also view broader data‑handling information in the Help Center: https://support.claude.com/en/collections/4078534-privacy-and-legal.
justification: Based on Section 4, the docs provide the exact robots.txt syntax to block ClaudeBot and the contact email for follow‑up. No other source is needed.

response:
To set up a Claude LTI key for your students, follow these steps:

**1. Create the Claude LTI developer key in Canvas**
1. Sign in to Canvas as an administrator and go to **Admin → Developer Keys**.
2. Click **+ Developer Key** then **+ LTI Key**.
3. Fill in the fields:
   - **Key Name:** Claude LTI
   - **Description:** (short description for the Claude LTI 1.3 app)
   - **Redirect URIs:** https://claude.ai/lti/launch
   - **Title:** Claude LTI
   - **Target Link URI:** https://claude.ai/lti/launch
   - **OpenID Connect Initiation Url:** https://claude.ai/api/lti/login
   - **JWK method:** https://claude.ai/api/lti/keys
4. Under **Additional Settings**, set **Privacy Level** to **Public**.
5. In **Placements**, remove the defaults and add **Course Navigation** and **Assignment Edit**.
6. Click **Save** and toggle the key **On**.

**2. Install Claude LTI as an app**
1. In Canvas, navigate to **Admin → Settings → Apps**.
2. Click **View App Configurations** then **+ App**.
3. Choose **Configuration Type: By Client ID**.
4. Enter the **Client ID** generated from the developer key you created in step 1.
5. Click **Install** and refresh the course page.

**3. Enable the integration in Claude for Education**
1. Sign in to Claude for Education as an administrator.
2. Go to **Organization settings > Connectors** (https://claude.ai/admin-settings/connectors).
3. Find **Canvas** and click **Enable**.
4. In the modal, enter:
   - **Canvas Domain**
   - **Client ID** (from Canvas Admin → Developer Keys)
   - **Deployment ID** (found in Canvas Admin → Settings → Apps → View App Configurations → Claude LTI Settings → Deployment ID)
5. Click **Save Changes**.

After these steps, the Claude LTI integration will be active for your courses and students.

If you have any further questions, contact your university’s administrator.
justification: Steps for creating the Claude LTI developer key and installing the app are taken from Section 1 (Creating Claude LTI Developer Key in Canvas) and Section 2 (Installing Claude LTI as an App) of the Claude for Education documentation.

response: In the US Virgin Islands, merchants are allowed to require a minimum purchase amount of US $10 for Visa credit card transactions. This is an exception to the general rule that merchants cannot set minimum or maximum amounts. If you encounter a problem because a merchant is enforcing this minimum, you should notify your Visa card issuer for assistance.
justification: Section 1 of visa\support.md states that in the USA and US territories (including the US Virgin Islands) a merchant may require a minimum transaction amount of US$10 for credit cards. It also advises notifying the Visa card issuer if you have an issue with a merchant's minimum spend requirement.
